description: Compile-time refinement violation on a statically-known literal (§53.11 E-CONTRACT-001). The cell type number(>0 && <10000) is initialized with the literal -50, which demonstrably fails the `value > 0` condition. The compiler proves the violation statically and fires E-CONTRACT-001 (the §34 error partition) — the compile half of refinement enforcement.
rationale: §53.11 E-CONTRACT-001 fires when the compiler can prove at compile time that a literal/statically-known value violates an inline predicate. -50 fails value>0.
